symbols: staff/spear — military authority as commander of the heavenly armies
orb with chi-rho monogram — dominion over the world under Christ's authority
large dark wings — angelic nature and supernatural power
halo — sanctity and closeness to God
red and blue robes — dual nature of divine love (red) and heavenly authority (blue)
frontal gaze — direct spiritual encounter and vigilant guardianship
Greek inscription — the sacred naming tradition of Orthodox iconography
description: A Byzantine icon on wood panel depicting the Archangel Michael in frontal half-length view. The angel has a youthful face with large solemn eyes, framed by dark hair and a golden halo. Large dark wings spread behind the figure. Michael wears layered robes in red and deep blue-black. In his right hand he holds a long staff or spear, and in his left hand he holds a round orb or disk bearing what appears to be a chi-rho monogram or other sacred letters. Greek inscription text appears at the top of the panel. The icon shows signs of age with paint loss and cracking. The background is golden-brown.
theological_description: The Archangel Michael, whose name means 'Who is like God?', serves as the supreme commander of the heavenly armies in biblical tradition. Daniel 10:13 identifies him as 'one of the chief princes,' and Daniel 12:1 describes him as 'the great prince who has charge of your people.' In Jude 9, Michael contends with the devil over the body of Moses. The definitive passage is Revelation 12:7-9: 'Now war arose in heaven, Michael and his angels fighting against the dragon... and the great dragon was thrown down, that ancient serpent.' The icon depicts Michael with the two attributes of his dual role: the staff of military command and the orb of cosmic authority under Christ (indicated by the chi-rho). The frontal presentation in Byzantine style conveys Michael's role as guardian and intercessor — he faces the viewer directly as protector. The large dark wings signify supernatural power, while the solemn, watchful eyes express perpetual vigilance against evil. Michael embodies the assurance that the forces of heaven are arrayed against the powers of darkness, and that the outcome of the cosmic battle is already determined by God's sovereign will.

scripture_refs.reason: Michael and his angels fighting the dragon in heaven — the definitive biblical role of the archangel
Michael identified as one of the chief princes who came to help in spiritual warfare
